info.title: IoT DC3 REST API
info.description:
IoT DC3 platform REST API documentation.

Covers all service modules: auth (authentication and authorization),
manager (device/driver/point/profile configuration), data (telemetry,
events, commands, notifications), and agentic (AI chat and tools).

Authentication: obtain a salt and token via /api/v3/auth/token/*, then
use the Authorize button to set X-Auth-Tenant, X-Auth-Login, and
X-Auth-Token. X-Auth-Token is a JSON object {"salt":"...","token":"..."}.

tags.name: label
tags.description: Label definitions: create, update, and manage labels for categorizing and tagging devices, drivers, and other platform entities with flexible key-value metadata

tags.name: event_attribute_config
tags.description: Event attribute configuration values: set and update per-device customization values for event properties inherited from event attribute definitions

tags.name: command
tags.description: Device command definitions: manage industrial device operations including read, write, and configuration commands with parameter specifications

tags.name: dictionary_manager
tags.description: Device-management dictionaries: manage lookup entries for driver types, device categories, point classifications, and other operational metadata

tags.name: event_param
tags.description: Event parameter definitions: manage data payload specifications for device events including name, type, and value mapping

tags.name: command_attribute
tags.description: Command attribute definitions: manage configurable parameters of device commands including name, type, default value, and validation rules

tags.name: label_bind
tags.description: Label tag bindings: assign and remove labels on devices, drivers, and other entities to enable categorization and filtered queries

tags.name: profile
tags.description: Device profile templates: manage reusable configuration bundles that combine driver settings, data point definitions, and command templates for rapid device onboarding

tags.name: event
tags.description: Device event definitions: manage alarm definitions, state-change notifications, and status events with configurable trigger conditions and severity levels

tags.name: command_param
tags.description: Command parameter definitions: manage input parameters for device commands including name, data type, and validation rules

tags.name: driver_attribute
tags.description: Driver attribute definitions: manage configurable properties of protocol drivers including name, type, default value, and validation constraints

tags.name: driver_attribute_config
tags.description: Driver attribute configuration values: set and update per-device customization values for driver properties inherited from driver attribute definitions

tags.name: topic
tags.description: Message topic definitions: manage named routing channels for device data and command messages flowing through the platform message bus

tags.name: device
tags.description: Industrial device lifecycle: register, configure, and manage device connectivity including driver assignment, topic binding, and operational status tracking

tags.name: command_attribute_config
tags.description: Command attribute configuration values: set and update per-device customization values for command properties inherited from command attribute definitions

tags.name: point
tags.description: Data point definitions: manage measurable or controllable attributes of industrial devices including read/write mode, data type, and value range

tags.name: group
tags.description: Logical group hierarchy: create, update, and manage groups for organizing devices, drivers, and platform resources into hierarchical collections

tags.name: point_attribute_config
tags.description: Point attribute configuration values: set and update per-device customization values for data point properties inherited from point attribute definitions

tags.name: driver
tags.description: Protocol driver lifecycle: register, configure, schedule, and control industrial protocol adapters that connect physical devices to the platform

tags.name: group_bind
tags.description: Group membership bindings: associate devices, drivers, and other entities with logical groups for hierarchical organization and bulk operations

tags.name: dashboard
tags.description: Manager dashboard configuration: manage device-management dashboard layouts, widgets, and display preferences

tags.name: event_attribute
tags.description: Event attribute definitions: manage configurable properties of device events including name, type, and value constraints

tags.name: point_attribute
tags.description: Point attribute definitions: manage configurable properties of data points including name, type, default value, and validation rules

post.description: Page through broker topics mapped to their device and point for the current tenant. Filter by topic name or device name to resolve which device and point a message topic is bound to; returns a page of topic bindings.
Modify an existing profile template's metadata (tenant-scoped). Only profile fields in the body change; points, commands, events and attributes are managed on their own endpoints.
Page through profile templates for the current tenant with the filters in the query body. Returns a page of profiles; use for browsing or selecting a template for a device.
Resolve a set of profile IDs to their profile templates for the current tenant. Returns a map of ID to profile; missing or foreign-tenant IDs are omitted.
Permanently delete a profile template by ID (tenant-scoped). The profile must belong to the current tenant; deletion cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Register a new profile template for the current tenant. A profile is a reusable template bundling points, commands, events and attributes that devices instantiate; returns the new profile ID.
Change the configured value of an existing point attribute config (tenant-scoped). Use to revise how a specific device-point pair reads or writes a point attribute through its driver.
Page through point attribute configs for the current tenant with query filters. Returns a page of configs; use for browsing or locating a config when the device or point binding is unknown.
Permanently delete a point attribute config by ID (tenant-scoped). Removes the configured value bound to a device-point pair; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Set the configured value of a point attribute on a specific device-point pair for the current tenant. A point attribute config is the concrete value of a field declared on the profile template; returns the new config ID.
Modify an existing point attribute field definition by ID (tenant-scoped). Use to change a driver-level field's name, code, type, default value or enable flag; ownership is verified before saving.

Page through point attribute field definitions for the current tenant with filters such as attribute name, driver and enable flag. Returns a page of point attributes; use for browsing or selecting a target attribute.
Permanently delete a point attribute field definition by ID (tenant-scoped). Removes the attribute from its driver; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Declare a new point attribute field for the current tenant on a given driver. A point attribute is a configurable field definition that tells the driver how to read or write a point's value; returns the new attribute ID.

Modify the definition of an existing point (name, data type, unit, access mode and similar fields) on its profile template. Changes apply to every device that instantiates the profile; tenant ownership is verified before mutating.
Resolve the engineering unit (for example Celsius or percent) of each point in a batch of IDs (tenant-scoped). Returns a map of point ID to unit string; only tenant-owned points are included.

Page through points for the current tenant with filters such as name and profile. Returns a page of point definitions; use for browsing points or selecting targets for value reads, writes or device binding.

Resolve a batch of point IDs to their definitions for the current tenant. Returns a map of point ID to point VO; IDs the tenant does not own are filtered out, so callers should treat missing keys as not-found.
Permanently remove a point from its profile template by ID (tenant-scoped). The point definition is removed from every device that instantiates the profile; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Define a new point (a single measurable channel such as a temperature reading) on a profile template for the current tenant. Points are later attached to devices and read or written through the driver; returns the new point ID.
Modify an existing label binding for the current tenant, such as re-pointing it to another label or entity. The new label and entity must match the binding's entity type and tenant scope.
Page through label bindings for the current tenant with filters from the query body. Returns a page of bindings; use to discover which entities carry a given label.
Remove a label binding by ID (tenant-scoped). Deletes only the association, leaving the label and the bound entity intact.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Attach a label to an entity (device, driver, etc.) for the current tenant. The label and target entity must share the same entity type and belong to the tenant; returns the add result.
Update an existing label's fields for the current tenant. Ownership is verified before applying the change; returns a success result.
Page through labels for the current tenant with filters from the query body. Returns a page of labels; use to browse available tags or pick one to apply to an entity.
Permanently delete a label by ID (tenant-scoped). The label is removed from the tenant; entities previously tagged with it are unaffected but lose the tag association. This action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Create a label for the current tenant. A label is a tag used to filter and organize devices, drivers, points and other entities; returns a success result.
Change the group or entity referenced by an existing binding (tenant-scoped). The new entity's type must still match the target group's type.

Page through group bindings for the current tenant, optionally filtered by group ID, entity type or entity ID. Returns a page of bindings; use to enumerate the members of a group or the groups an entity belongs to.
Remove a group-to-entity binding by ID (tenant-scoped). Detaches the entity from the group without deleting the group or the entity.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Attach a tenant entity (device, driver, point, etc.) to a group. The entity's type must match the group's type and ownership is tenant-scoped; returns the new binding ID.
Update an existing group's attributes for the current tenant. Validates tenant ownership before applying the change; returns the updated group ID.
Page through groups for the current tenant with optional query filters. Returns a page of groups; use for browsing available groupings or selecting a target group.
Permanently delete a group by ID (tenant-scoped). Removes the grouping definition without deleting its member entities; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Create a group for the current tenant. A group is a logical grouping of devices, drivers, points or other entities used for batch operations; returns the new group ID.
Modify an existing event param's name, code, type or extension (tenant-scoped, ownership verified before mutation). Use to correct a parameter declared on an event.
Page through event params for the current tenant with filters from the query body. Returns a page of event params; use for browsing or auditing parameter declarations across events.
Permanently delete an event param by ID (tenant-scoped, ownership verified before deletion). The parameter is removed from its event; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Declare a new parameter on an event for the current tenant. An event param is a named field an event exposes (with type and extension); returns the new event param ID.
Change the configured value of an existing event attribute field for a specific device and event under the current tenant. Tenant ownership of the record is verified before applying the update.
Page through event attribute configurations for the current tenant with filters from the query body. Returns a page of configurations; use for browsing or selecting a target configuration.

Permanently delete one event attribute configuration by ID (tenant-scoped). Removes the device's configured value for that attribute while leaving the attribute definition on the profile template intact; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Set the configured value of one event attribute field for a specific device and event under the current tenant. Use to override the attribute definition declared on the profile template for that device instance; returns the new config ID.
Modify an existing event attribute field definition on its profile template. Tenant-scoped: ownership of the existing attribute is verified before applying the change.
Page through event attribute field definitions for the current tenant with filters from the query body. Returns a page of attributes; use for browsing or selecting a target event attribute.
Permanently delete an event attribute field definition by ID (tenant-scoped). Removes the attribute from its profile template; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Define a new event attribute field on a profile template for the current tenant. An event attribute declares one configurable field of a device-reported event; returns the new attribute ID.
Modify an existing event definition on the profile template (tenant-scoped). Updates event attributes such as name and configuration; use to refine how a device-reported occurrence is modeled.
Page through event definitions for the current tenant with filters from the query body. Returns a page of events; use for browsing or locating a specific event definition.

Permanently delete an event definition by ID (tenant-scoped). Removes the event and its attribute definitions from the profile template while preserving reported event history; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Define a new device-reported event on a profile template for the current tenant. An event is an occurrence or alert a device raises at runtime, with its attribute definitions; returns the new event ID.
Change the configured value of an existing driver attribute config (tenant-scoped). Use to reconfigure how a specific device's driver field is set; ownership is verified before the update is applied.
Page through driver attribute configs for the current tenant with query filters. Returns a page of configured driver attribute values; use for browsing or selecting a target config.
Permanently delete a driver attribute config by ID (tenant-scoped). Removes the configured value for that driver attribute on its device; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Set the configured value of a driver attribute for a device under the current tenant. A driver attribute config holds the concrete value of a field declared on the device's driver; returns the new config ID.
Update an existing driver attribute (tenant-scoped). Modifies the field definition's name, code, type, default value or enable flag on its driver.
Page through driver attributes for the current tenant with filters such as name, code and driver. Returns a page of driver attributes; use for browsing or selecting a target attribute.
Permanently delete a driver attribute by ID (tenant-scoped). Removes the attribute definition for its driver; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Define a new driver attribute for the current tenant. A driver attribute is a configurable field declared on a driver (name, code, type, default value) that driver instances supply a concrete value for; returns the new attribute ID.
Update an existing driver's attributes (tenant-scoped). Modifies the protocol adapter configuration such as service name, mode and enable flag; returns the update result.

Page through drivers for the current tenant with filters such as name, service name, mode and enable flag. Returns a page of drivers; use for browsing or selecting a target protocol adapter for a device.
Return the drivers matching a set of IDs, filtered to the current tenant. Returns a map of driver ID to driver; missing or out-of-tenant IDs are silently omitted.
Permanently delete a driver by ID (tenant-scoped). Removes the driver adapter; devices bound to it can no longer collect or write point values until reassigned; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Register a new driver protocol adapter for the current tenant. A driver connects devices to the platform and reads or writes their point values; returns the new driver ID.
Page through profile template dictionaries for the current tenant. Use to select which profile template a device instantiates; returns a page of profile dictionaries.
Page through the point dictionaries declared on a given profile template. Use to discover which measurable channels a profile exposes; the query must carry a valid profile parent id.
Page through driver dictionary entries for the current tenant. Use to pick a driver (protocol adapter) when registering or reassigning a device; returns a page of driver dictionaries.
Page through the device dictionaries managed by a given driver. Use to find which devices a protocol adapter is responsible for; the query must carry a valid driver parent id.
Page through device dictionaries for the current tenant. Use to select a target device for value reads, commands or configuration; returns a page of device dictionaries.
Page through the point dictionaries available on a given device. Use to pick a target point before reading values or sending commands; the query must carry a valid device parent id.
Modify an existing device's attributes such as name, profile, driver and connection settings. Tenant ownership is verified before applying the update.
Page through devices for the current tenant with filters such as name, profile, driver and enable flag. Returns a page of devices; use for browsing or selecting a target device.
Resolve a batch of device IDs to their details for the current tenant. Returns a map of device ID to device VO; IDs the tenant does not own are filtered out, so treat missing keys as not-found.
Bulk-create devices for the current tenant by uploading an XLSX file (max 20 MB) shaped by the import template; each row becomes a device under the supplied profile and driver.

post.description: Generate and download the XLSX template used for bulk device import, pre-shaped for the supplied profile and driver. Fill it in and upload it to the import endpoint.
Permanently delete a device by ID (tenant-scoped). Removes the device and its point-value configuration while preserving collected history; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Register a new IoT device for the current tenant. A device is a physical or virtual data source that collects point values through a driver; returns the device ID.
Modify an existing command parameter's name, code, direction, type, required flag or default value. Verifies ownership against the current tenant before applying the change.

Page through command parameters for the current tenant with filters such as name, code, command and enable flag. Returns a page of command parameters; use for browsing or auditing parameter definitions across commands.
Permanently delete a command parameter by ID (tenant-scoped). Removes the parameter definition from its parent command; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Define a new input or output parameter on a command for the current tenant. A command parameter declares the name, code, direction, type and default value that a downward device command accepts or returns; returns the new parameter ID.
Change the configured value of an existing command attribute for a specific device and command. Verifies the record belongs to the current tenant before applying the update.

Page through command attribute configurations for the current tenant with filters from the query body. Returns a page of configurations; use for browsing or auditing configured command attribute values across devices and commands.
Permanently delete one command attribute configuration by ID (tenant-scoped). The device reverts to the attribute's default declared on the profile template; the deletion cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Set the concrete value of a command attribute field for a specific device and command under the current tenant. The configured value overrides the field's default declared on the profile template; returns the new configuration ID.
Modify an existing command attribute field definition (tenant-scoped). Use to rename or change the type/default of a field declared on a command in the profile template.

Page through command attribute field definitions for the current tenant with query filters. Returns a page of attributes; use for browsing command fields or selecting a target attribute to inspect or edit.
Permanently delete a command attribute field definition by ID (tenant-scoped). Removes the field from its parent command; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Declare a new command attribute field on a profile template. A command attribute is a configurable field definition of a downward control instruction; returns the new attribute ID.
Modify an existing command's parameters and attributes (tenant-scoped). Ownership is verified before applying changes; returns an update-success response.
Page through control commands for the current tenant with query filters. Returns a page of commands; use for browsing commands or selecting one to send to a device.
Permanently delete a command by ID (tenant-scoped). Removes the command definition from its profile template; the action cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the entity to delete. Must belong to the current tenant.

post.description: Create a downward control instruction defined on a profile template for the current tenant. A command carries parameters and attributes that the driver sends to a device; returns the new command ID.

get.description: Return every profile template instantiated by a given device (tenant-scoped). Use to discover which point, command and event definitions a device exposes through its driver.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose instantiated profiles are returned; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one profile template by ID (tenant-scoped). Use to inspect a profile's metadata before binding devices or editing its point, command and event definitions.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: List Point Attribute Configurations by Device ID
get.description: Return every point attribute config for one device across all its points (tenant-scoped). Returns a flat list; use to review the full configuration surface of a device at once.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose point attribute configs are listed; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: List Point Attribute Configurations by Device and Point IDs
get.description: Return every point attribute config bound to one device-point pair (tenant-scoped). Use to see all configured attribute values that govern how that point is read or written on the device.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose point configs are listed; must belong to the current tenant.

parameters.name: point_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the data point whose attribute configs are listed; must share its profile with the device.

get.summary: Get Point Attribute Configuration by ID
get.description: Fetch one point attribute config by its record ID (tenant-scoped). Use to inspect the configured value bound to a device-point pair before updating or deleting it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: Get Point Attribute Configuration by Attribute, Device, and Point IDs
get.description: Fetch the single config that applies one point attribute to a specific device-point pair. Look up by the (attribute, device, point) tuple; returns the configured value for that exact binding.

parameters.name: attribute_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the point attribute definition whose configured value is being looked up; must belong to the current tenant's driver.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device the config applies to; must belong to the current tenant and share its profile with the point.

parameters.name: point_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the data point the config applies to; must belong to the current tenant and share its profile with the device.

get.summary: List Point Attributes by Driver ID
get.description: Return every point attribute field definition owned by a given driver (tenant-scoped). Use to discover which configurable fields a driver exposes for reading or writing point values; returns an empty list when the driver is not found.

parameters.name: driver_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the driver whose point attribute field definitions are returned; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one point attribute field definition by ID (tenant-scoped). Use to inspect a driver-level field such as its name, code, type, default value and enable flag before editing it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return device-level statistics for a single point, such as how many devices expose it (tenant-scoped). Use to gauge the blast radius of editing a point before changing its definition; the point must belong to the tenant.

parameters.name: point_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the point whose device statistics should be returned; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return every point defined on a given profile template (tenant-scoped). Use to enumerate the measurable channels a device will expose once it instantiates the profile; the profile must belong to the tenant.

parameters.name: profile_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the profile template whose points should be listed; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return every point available on a specific device (tenant-scoped). These are the channels the bound driver can read or write for that device; the device must belong to the tenant.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose available points should be listed; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch the resolved point configuration for a device, merging the profile template definitions with the device's per-instance attribute values (tenant-scoped). Use to see exactly how each point is configured before issuing reads, writes or commands through the driver.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose resolved point configuration should be returned; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return the number of points available on a specific device (tenant-scoped). Use for quick cardinality checks without fetching full definitions; the device must belong to the tenant.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose point count should be returned; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch a single point's definition (data type, unit, access mode and metadata) by ID. Tenant-scoped; use to inspect a point before reading its values or binding it to a device.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one label binding by ID (tenant-scoped). Use to inspect which label is attached to which entity and its entity type.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one label by ID (tenant-scoped). Use to inspect a label's name and color before applying it to or filtering entities.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one group binding by ID (tenant-scoped). Returns the group ID, entity type and entity ID of the association; use to inspect a specific link before editing or removing it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one group by ID for the current tenant. Use to inspect a grouping definition before assigning entities to it or performing batch operations.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: List Event Parameters by Event ID
get.description: Return every parameter declared on a given event (tenant-scoped, event ownership verified). Use to discover which fields a device-reported event exposes.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the event whose parameters are listed; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one event param with its name, code, type and extension. Use to inspect a parameter a device-reported event exposes before reading event values.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: List Event Attribute Configurations by Device ID
get.description: Return every event attribute configuration for one device across all of its events (tenant-scoped). Use to read the full set of configured event-attribute values a device uses.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose configurations are listed; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: List Event Attribute Configurations by Device and Event IDs
get.description: Return every event attribute configuration for one device and one event (tenant-scoped). Use to read all configured values the device supplies for that event's attributes; the device's profile must match the event.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose configurations are listed; must belong to the current tenant.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the event whose configurations are listed; its profile must match the device's profile.

get.summary: Get Event Attribute Configuration by ID
get.description: Fetch one event attribute configuration by its record ID (tenant-scoped). Use to inspect the configured value a device uses for a single event attribute field.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: Get Event Attribute Configuration by Attribute, Device, and Event IDs
get.description: Fetch the configured value of one event attribute field for a specific device, event, and attribute. Validates that the device's profile matches the event and its driver matches the attribute before returning; use when you need a single attribute's device-specific override.

parameters.name: attribute_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the event attribute whose configured value is being fetched; its driver must match the device's driver.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose configured value is being fetched; must belong to the current tenant.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the event whose attribute value is being fetched; its profile must match the device's profile.

get.summary: List Event Attributes by Driver ID
get.description: Return every event attribute field definition reachable through a given driver (tenant-scoped). Use to discover which device-reported event fields a driver exposes; returns an empty list when the driver is not found.

parameters.name: driver_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the driver whose reachable event attribute fields should be listed; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one event attribute field definition by ID (tenant-scoped). Use to inspect a single configurable field of a device-reported event before editing it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return every event definition declared on a given profile template (tenant-scoped). Use to discover which device-reported occurrences the profile exposes before binding or inspecting a device.

parameters.name: profile_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the profile template whose event definitions should be returned; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return every event definition available on a given device (tenant-scoped), resolved from the device's profile template. Use to see which occurrences a specific device may report.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose available event definitions should be returned; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one event definition with its attribute definitions (tenant-scoped). Use to inspect an event before subscribing to its reported history or adjusting its configuration.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: List Driver Attribute Configurations by Device ID
get.description: Return every driver attribute config for a given device (tenant-scoped). Use to load all connection attribute values a device applies to its driver in one call.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose driver attribute configs are being listed; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: Get Driver Attribute Configuration by ID
get.description: Fetch one driver attribute config by ID (tenant-scoped). Returns the configured value of a single driver attribute field for the device it belongs to.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: Get Driver Attribute Configuration by Device and Attribute IDs
get.description: Fetch the configured value of one driver attribute for a specific device (tenant-scoped). The device's driver must match the attribute's driver; use to read a single connection attribute before invoking the driver.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose driver attribute config is being read; must belong to the current tenant.

parameters.name: attribute_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the driver attribute whose configured value is being read; its driver must match the device's driver.

get.summary: List Driver Attributes by Driver ID
get.description: Return every driver attribute declared on a given driver (tenant-scoped). Use to discover which configurable fields a driver exposes; returns an empty list when the driver does not exist.

parameters.name: driver_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the driver whose declared attributes are returned; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one driver attribute by ID (tenant-scoped). Use to inspect a driver's configurable field definition such as its type and default value before configuring driver attribute values.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one driver by its protocol service name (tenant-scoped). Use to resolve a driver instance from the service identifier under which it registered with the platform.

parameters.name: service_name
parameters.description: Unique protocol service name under which the driver registered; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one driver by ID (tenant-scoped). Use to inspect a protocol adapter before assigning devices, sending commands or reading point values through it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return every device that instantiates a given profile template (tenant-scoped). Use to find which devices share the same point, command and event definitions.

parameters.name: profile_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the profile template whose instantiated devices are returned; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return how many devices for the current tenant are driven by a given driver. Use for quick cardinality checks before reconfiguring a driver; the driver must belong to the tenant.

parameters.name: driver_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the driver whose device count is returned; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one device with its bound profile, driver and connection attributes. Use to inspect a device before sending commands or reading its point values.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Build the Driver → Device → Profile → Point topology for the current tenant. Cardinality mode (default) counts relationships along each edge; the range_key parameter selects a preset time window. Returns a Sankey graph with server-side top-N cropping and others pseudo-nodes carrying hidden children for drill-in.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Topology aggregation mode: cardinality counts relationships per edge (default), volume weights edges by point-value sample counts over the range window.

parameters.name: range_key
parameters.description: Preset time window for volume mode: today, 24h, 7d, or 30d. Ignored in cardinality mode.

get.description: Return daily new-row counts for driver, device, point and profile tables over the trailing days window (default 7). Tenant-scoped and zero-padded to a fixed length per resource so missing days appear as zero; backs the stat-card sparklines.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Trailing day window for the growth trend; output is zero-padded to this length.

get.description: Aggregate driver statistics for the current tenant for the dashboard home view. Returns driver counts grouped by enable status and protocol type; use to surface driver distribution across the tenant.

Aggregate device statistics for the current tenant for the dashboard home view. Returns device counts plus the top-N devices by point-value volume, where N is set by the top_n parameter (default 10); use to surface device distribution and the most active devices.

parameters.name: top_n
parameters.description: Number of top devices to return, ranked by point-value volume; clamped server-side.

get.summary: List Command Parameters by Command ID
get.description: Return every parameter declared on a given command, tenant-scoped. Use to discover the input and output parameters a downward device command accepts or returns when building or validating a command call.

parameters.name: command_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the command whose parameters are listed; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one command parameter with its direction, type, required flag and default value. Use to inspect what an input or output parameter of a downward device command looks like before issuing or interpreting the command.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: List Command Attribute Configurations by Device ID
get.description: Return every command attribute configuration set on a specific device across all of its commands, scoped to the current tenant. Use to review the full configuration of a device before sending commands.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose command attribute configurations are being listed; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: List Command Attribute Configurations by Device and Command IDs
get.description: Return every command attribute configuration set on a specific device for a specific command, scoped to the current tenant. Use to retrieve the full set of values that device will send with that command.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose command attribute configurations are being listed; must belong to the current tenant.

parameters.name: command_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the command whose attribute configurations are being listed; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: Get Command Attribute Configuration by ID
get.description: Fetch one command attribute configuration by its record ID. Use to inspect the value a specific device applies to a command's attribute field, scoped to the current tenant.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: Get Command Attribute Configuration by Attribute, Device, and Command IDs
get.description: Fetch the configured value of one command attribute field by its attribute, device and command IDs. Use when the configuration ID is unknown but the attribute-device-command triple is, scoped to the current tenant.

parameters.name: attribute_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the command attribute field whose configured value is being looked up; must belong to the current tenant.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose configured command attribute value is being looked up; must belong to the current tenant.

parameters.name: command_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the command whose attribute configuration is being looked up; must belong to the current tenant.

get.summary: List Command Attributes by Driver ID
get.description: Return every command attribute exposed by the commands of devices driven by a given driver (tenant-scoped). Use to enumerate which configurable command fields a driver-type adapter can send; returns an empty list when the driver is not found.

parameters.name: driver_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the driver whose command attributes are enumerated; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one command attribute field definition by ID (tenant-scoped). Returns the attribute's name, type and default value as declared on its parent command.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return every control command declared on a given profile template (tenant-scoped). Use to enumerate the downward instructions available to all devices that instantiate the profile.

parameters.name: profile_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the profile template whose commands are returned; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Return the control commands a given device can receive, resolved from its bound profile template (tenant-scoped). Use to discover which downward instructions can be sent to a specific device through its driver.

parameters.name: device_id
parameters.description: Identifier of the device whose receivable commands are returned; must belong to the current tenant.

get.description: Fetch one command with its parameters and attributes (tenant-scoped). Use to inspect a command before sending it to a device through the driver.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Primary key of the target record; must belong to the current tenant.

TopologyHiddenChildVO.description: An entity collapsed into an others bucket, retaining enough info to route on click

id.description: Prefixed id (driver:{n}, device:{n} or point:{n})

name.description: Display name of the collapsed entity

type.description: Entity type: driver, device or point (the profile layer is never collapsed)

TopologyLinkVO.description: One edge in the topology Sankey graph

source.description: Prefixed identifier of the source node, formatted as '<type>:<id>' (e.g. driver, device, profile, point); both endpoints are scoped to the current tenant.

target.description: Prefixed identifier of the target node, formatted as '<type>:<id>' (e.g. driver, device, profile, point); both endpoints are scoped to the current tenant.

value.description: Edge weight: in cardinality mode the number of relationships this edge represents (1 per device for Driver→Device, 1 per profile_bind for Device→Profile, 1 per point for Profile→Point, aggregated into N for '*→others'); in volume mode the rolled-up point_value sample count.

TopologyNodeVO.description: One node in the topology Sankey graph

id.description: Prefixed id (driver:{n}, device:{n}, profile:{n}, point:{n} or others:{layer}:{parentId})

name.description: Human-readable label; for others:* nodes it is "Others (N)" where N is the hidden child count

type.description: Node type: driver, device, profile, point or others

hiddenChildren.description: Entities collapsed into this node; populated only on others:* nodes

TopologyStatsVO.description: Tenant-wide total counts for the topology footer summary

driverCount.description: Total number of drivers for the tenant

deviceCount.description: Total number of devices for the tenant

profileCount.description: Total number of profiles for the tenant

pointCount.description: Total number of points for the tenant

rangeLabel.description: Short human-readable range label (e.g. 24h, 7d); present only in volume mode, null in cardinality mode

TopologyVO.description: Topology payload: a 4-column Sankey graph (Driver to Device to Profile to Point)

nodes.description: Sankey nodes across the driver, device, profile and point columns

stats.description: Tenant-wide total counts used for the footer summary

GrowthVO.description: Daily new-row counts per entity for the home page sparklines (zero-padded, oldest first)
